title: Gift Card Buying

title: Lotus Cars

title: Car Sun Protection

title: Small Boats

title: Passenger Car Regulations
description: What are the recent changes to energy labeling and emission targets for new passenger cars in the EU, and how do they impact vehicle classification and manufacturing trends?
narrative: The user is looking for up‐to‑date, regulatory information concerning new passenger cars in the European market. Relevance criteria include documents that describe the transition from NCEC to WLTP testing, the redefinition of target CO₂ emission values, the redesign of the energy label (target value visibility, category limits), weight and design trends affecting emissions, and any official policy announcements from EU agencies or national energy offices. Documents that merely discuss rentals, vehicle types unrelated to regulation, or unrelated travel content are irrelevant.

title: War School History
description: Which historical institutions have functioned as "war schools," and how were they used during and after conflict periods?
narrative: The user seeks a comprehensive view of schools that were explicitly named or conceptualized as war schools, encompassing both military training establishments (e.g., wartime officers’ schools, war colleges) and civilian educational initiatives set up in the aftermath of major wars to serve displaced or marginalized communities. Relevance is judged by the presence of discussions on the founding, curriculum, target population, and wartime or post‑war roles of such institutions; documents that merely mention war or schools without linking them to a war‑school context are excluded. Likewise, purely modern educational institutions unrelated to wartime activities are considered non‑relevant.

title: Pole Emploi Right of Option
description: What is the ‘right of option’ at Pole Emploi, who is eligible, and how does it affect my unemployment benefits?
narrative: The user seeks a concise, policy‑focused explanation of the Pole Emploi right of option, including eligibility criteria, the calculation of the higher but shorter allowances, the conditions that trigger it, and the administrative steps to request it. Relevance requires documents that discuss the option’s definition, eligibility conditions (e.g., previous unexhausted rights, new allowance at least 30 % higher or €20/day), the effect on total compensation, and the request procedure (writing to Pole Emploi, 21‑day cooling‑off). Non‑relevant content such as attestation guides or general employment return procedures is excluded. The narrative should clarify that this option is intended to maximize payouts for unemployed individuals who return to higher‑paying jobs or start a business, while noting the permanent loss of older, lower‑rate benefits.}
